etymology.description: 家(家) (jiā, "home; family") refers to one's house or the family residing there, capturing the sense of a personal base; 鄉(鄉) (xiāng, "rural; hometown") denotes a village or rural area where one may have origins or familial connections.
etymology.interpretation: 家(家) (jiā, "home; family") conveys the idea of an individual's central living space or familial nucleus. 鄉(鄉) (xiāng, "hometown") implies one's place of origin, associated with rural roots or local communities. Thus, 家鄉(家鄉) (jiāxiāng, "hometown") brings together the concept of a family base and local origins, capturing the emotional and historical ties to one's birthplace or formative environment.

examples.chinese: 我的家乡在一个美丽的山谷(我的家鄉在一個美麗的山谷)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ de jiāxiāng zài yīgè měilì de shāngǔ.
examples.english: My hometown is in a beautiful valley.

examples.chinese: 每年春节我都会回家乡(每年春節我都會回家鄉)。
examples.pinyin: Měinián chūnjié wǒ dūhuì huí jiāxiāng.
examples.english: Every Spring Festival, I return to my hometown.

examples.chinese: 他离开家乡去大城市工作(他離開家鄉去大城市工作)。
examples.pinyin: Tā líkāi jiāxiāng qù dà chéngshì gōngzuò.
examples.english: He left his hometown to work in the big city.
